findings.title: Swap invariant uses a 10,000-based fee adjustment against a 1,000-based RHS, allowing near-total reserve drainage

best_agent_match.title: Swap invariant is scaled 100x too low, enabling near-total reserve drainage

findings.title: Swaps are hard-coupled to an external referral contract, creating a single-point denial of service

best_agent_match.title: Every swap is hard-coupled to an external referral contract, creating a single-point DOS

findings.title: Factory can reinitialize an existing pair because initialization is not one-time

best_agent_match.title: Factory can reinitialize an existing pair because initialization is not one-time

findings.title: Any LP tokens held by the pair are permissionlessly redeemable, including misrouted protocol fees

best_agent_match.title: Any LP tokens sent to the pair contract can be burned and redeemed by anyone

rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan attack via callback + skim allows stealing pool funds
rejected_candidates.reason: `swap()` and `skim()` share the same `lock` modifier, so `skim()` cannot be reentered from `NimbusCall`; the proposed exploit path is blocked.

rejected_candidates.title: DOMAIN_SEPARATOR not updated on chain fork leading to signature reuse
rejected_candidates.reason: The code matches an older fixed-domain permit pattern, but the risk is fork-contingent and too speculative for a reportable issue in this audit.

rejected_candidates.title: Swap callback allows arbitrary external calls enabling potential attack vectors
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the standard flash-swap callback pattern; no concrete exploit survives the pair's reentrancy lock.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded magic number for infinite approval
rejected_candidates.reason: Readability issue only; not a security vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No check for zero-address in setFeeTo
rejected_candidates.reason: `setFeeTo` is not implemented in this contract, and a zero `feeTo` is the normal way to disable fees.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing sync before K check
rejected_candidates.reason: The invariant check uses live token balances read immediately before validation, so a prior `sync()` is unnecessary.

rejected_candidates.title: Anyone can steal tokens accidentally sent to the pair via skim
rejected_candidates.reason: `skim()` is an intentional Uniswap-style dust recovery mechanism that only transfers balances above recorded reserves; this is by design rather than a distinct vulnerability.
